{{Quote|The discovery of microbial life has halted corporate dumping on planetoid PDC3141-02, but has in turn endangered the crew of the Trade Federation scow, ''Spotty Conveyor''.|HoloNet News report|<ref name="HNN" />}}
'''PDC3141-02''' was a [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]] located in the [[Trax sector/Legends|Trax sector]]. For [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]], the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] used it for dumping [[Radiation/Legends|radioactive]] industrial waste. Shortly prior to [[22 BBY/Legends|13:3:21]], [[University of Sanbra]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] discovered a native unicellular [[Lifeform PDC3141-02|lifeform]] on the planetoid, and a subsequent temporary injunction prevented further Federation dumping. At the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the discovery, however, a [[Spotty Conveyor|Trade Federation scow]] had been transporting a cargo of hazardous waste to PDC3141-02. Since the injunction against dumping on the planetoid endangered the [[Garbage scow/Legends|vessel's]] crew, the Federation subsequently attempted to bypass the restriction.

==Description==
{{Quote|During its journey, scientists from the University of Sanbra discovered a strain of native unicellular life on what was presumed to be a lifeless planet.|HoloNet News report|<ref name="HNN" />}}
PDC3141-02 was a [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]]<ref name="HNN" /> located in the [[PDC3141-02 system/Legends|PDC3141-02 system]],<ref name="EAOC" /> a part of the [[Kashyyyk Region]]'s<ref name="Kashyyyk" /> [[Trax sector/Legends|Trax sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to a [[Lifeform PDC3141-02|strain]] of unicellular microbe.<ref name="HNN" />

==History==
{{Quote|This is an outrage! We have been using that planetoid for centuries. Surely one more deposit will not endanger this imagined lifeform.|Senator Lott Dod, in a HoloNet News report|<ref name="HNN" />}}
For [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] prior to the [[Great ReSynchronization|date]] [[22 BBY/Legends|13:3:21]], the conglomerate known as the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] used PDC3141-02 as dumping grounds for [[Radiation/Legends|radioactive]] industrial waste. At some point during the [[Standard week/Legends|week]] preceding that date, [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] from the [[University of Sanbra]] visited the supposedly lifeless planetoid and discovered its native microbe, which was accordingly named "Lifeform PDC3141-02." The University later obtained a temporary injunction restricting any further dumping on the planetoid until the researchers' readings could be verified.<ref name="HNN" />

[[File:Lott_Dodd_thumb.png|thumb|right|225px|Trade Federation Senator Lott Dod expressed his dissatisfaction with the injuction against corporate dumping on PDC3141-02.]]
At the same [[Time/Legends|time]], however, the [[Garbage scow/Legends|Trade Federation scow]] ''[[Spotty Conveyor]]'' had been traveling to PDC3141-02 with a load of radioactive waste from Federation [[Factory world/Legends|factory worlds]] in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]]. Upon its arrival in the planetoid's [[Star system/Legends|star system]], the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] discovered it was disallowed from depositing its hazardous cargo. Since the scow was not carrying enough [[Fuel/Legends|fuel]] to transport all of the waste back to its [[Spaceport/Legends|ports]] of origin and its [[rad-dampening shielding]] would only remain effective for five additional [[Standard day/Legends|days]], the safety of the vessel's crew became endangered.<ref name="HNN" />

In response, the Trade Federation lobbied to bypass the University's injunction against the dumping on PDC3141-02, with the organization's angered [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Lott Dod/Legends|Lott Dod]] proclaiming at the 13:3:21 session of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]]<ref name="HNN" /> his<ref name="DB Dod">{{DB|character|lottdod|Dod, Lott}}</ref> belief that the radioactive waste from the ''Spotty Conveyor'' posed no threat to the planetoid's microbes, the actual existence of which he remained skeptical. The senator also indignantly stated the theoretical possibility that the Federation's dumping on PDC3141-02 could have, in fact, benefitted the native lifeform. The incident on the planetoid was subsequently covered by an issue of the [[HoloNet News CoCo District Edition|CoCo District Edition]] of the [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]], which on one occasion referred to PDC3141-02 as a [[Planet/Legends|planet]].<ref name="HNN" />

==Behind the scenes==
PDC3141-02 was mentioned in [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 48|''HoloNet News Vol. 531 #48'']], an [[in-universe]] issue of the HoloNet News<ref name="HNN" /> written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and [[Paul Ens]]<ref name="Credits">{{HoloNetNewsWeb||credits.html|Credits}}</ref> and published on the [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News website]] on [[March 21]], [[2002]].<ref name="HNN48 date">{{JCFCite|url=posts/6128630|board=Literature|thread=The Official HoloNetNews Discussion Thread|user=PrinceXizor|date=2002-03-21|time=3:24 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200305125429/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-holonetnews-discussion-thread.5990764/page-13}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the PDC3141-02 system, and therefore the planetoid, in grid square P-10.<ref name="TEA" />
